title: When is quantization worth the added complexity?
prompt:
When is quantization worth the added complexity?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
Worth it when self-hosting models: 4-bit quantization lets you run a 14B model on your 48GB M4 Pro instead of being limited to 7B models at full precision. INT8 quantization typically loses 1-2% quality, INT4 loses 3-5% on benchmarks. Not worth it when using hosted APIs (the provider handles optimization) or when quality degradation is unacceptable for your specific task.

**Takeaway:** Quantize for self-hosting efficiency. INT8 for quality, INT4 for capability at constrained memory.
hint_notes: Quantize for self-hosting efficiency. INT8 for quality, INT4 for capability at constrained memory.
